channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Starfield Free Lanes Update Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Bethesda has finally revealed all the new features and changes coming to Starfield with the Free Lanes update.

Starfield developers, Bethesda, came in guns blazing earlier this month, finally unveiling the Terran Armada DLC, announcing the Free Lanes update, and providing players with a much-awaited PS5 release date. Now, just a week away from its release, the devs are providing more information about the Free Lanes update.

Starfield Free Lanes Update Introduces Cruise Mode, X-Tech, Inventory Management Improvements, And More

Cruise Mode

Bethesda explains that Starfield’s Free Lanes update is designed for interplanetary exploration, and with that, the first addition to the game is Cruise Mode. In Cruise Mode, your speed can increase to light-years, making it easier to fly between planets. The new mode lets players move freely around their ship while traveling, allowing them to do the same things they normally would. There’s also a new, unique encounter called Interdiction, which can interrupt Cruise Mode.

X-Tech

Next up is X-Tech, a new resource for customizing and upgrading the player’s weapons, gear, and ship. It allows players to re-roll legendary effects, and the Free Lanes update adds a new legendary rank for weapons, helmets, packs, and suits. The new effects include Saboteur, Reckless, and Enigmatic. Players can also use X-tech through the Ship Optimization Terminal to upgrade different systems like shield strength, weapons, engines, grav drive, and more

Improved Resource Management

Regarding improved resource management in the Starfield Free Lanes update, players can now install an outpost container at each outpost for easier access to resources. The new Database system pulls in everything the players have learned, including locations, recipes, resources, ingredients, and more. Lastly, you can become an alien pet owner with the Milliewhale outpost pet. The new character can perform tricks, can be customized, and more. Players need to unlock him by completing the Housesitting” quest in Anchorpoint.

New Starborn Abilities and Quantum Entanglement Device

Furthermore, players will receive new ways to upgrade their Starborn Abilities through Quantum Essence, without having to start a New Game+ and find temples. The Quantum Entanglement Device also uses Quantum Essence for players to bring their favorite inventory items to their new playthrough.

New Anchorpoint POI, Ship Modules, Vehicles, Side Quests, Much More

That’s not all, the latest blog post on the Starfield Free Lanes update details several new additions, including the Anchorpoint POI, other planetary POI, an Asteroid Base Home, and much more that players can learn about below:

  • Anchorpoint Station: Anchorpoint is a new star station with vendors and quests
  • New Ship Modules: New ship modules have been added to upgrade different ship specs and even add some unique new abilities to your ship, like a new stealth module that cloaks your ship while boosting. These modules will drop-in ship combat as schematics and can be installed by any ship services technician.
  • Upgrade Module Slot: You’ll notice as you’re customizing your gear that there’s a new Upgrade Module slot. This slot lets you upgrade a specific stat, like range, rate of fire, etc., on your gear.
  • Colony War Action Heroes: There are 27 Colony War Action Hero collectibles for you to track down. Each one gives a stat buff (with different buffs for opened and unopened versions) and will look great decorating your outpost in the new outpost display cases.
  • Many more planetary POIs: You’ll notice an increased variety in the types of planetary POIs as you explore.
  • New Elite Crew: Fan-favorite NPC Muria Siarkiewicz can now be added as an Elite Crewmember. Find her at her normal spot near Galbank in New Atlantis.
  • New side quests: There are several people on Anchorpoint who could use your help. Talk to everyone.
  • New land vehicle: Bounce your way across the surface in style with the new Moon Jumper land vehicle. With its impressive boost capabilities, the Moon Jumper is built for verticality, allowing you to reach high-up places much more easily while in a vehicle.
  • Asteroid base home: For those with a lot of credits to burn, you can now purchase a very ritzy asteroid base to call home. Explore Anchorpoint to learn how to purchase it.
  • New enemy modifiers: Add some extra challenge to combat with new enemy modifiers like Tank (3x more health), Tricky (chance to avoid damage), Martyr (dying puts nearby allies into a rage), and more. These modifiers can be tuned in the Gameplay Options menu. All new modifiers will be identified with new icons near enemy health bars, and full descriptions can be found in scanner mode.
  • New weapon skins: New Constellation and Neon skins for select weapons
  • Photomode poses with the REV-8
  • New camera features: A toggle to always play a landing camera has been added to Accessibility settings. Additionally, a more distant ship POV level has also been added to camera options.

In other news, early Starfield tests with DLSS 5 from GTC 2026 provide a glimpse at how Bethesda may integrate the tech into future updates. What are your thoughts on the Starfield Free Lanes update? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the latest addition to PlayStation Plus monthly games in April 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Where Is Adam Smasher in Fortnite? Cyberpunk 2077 Crossover Skin Release Date, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Cyberpunk 2077 has been welcomed with open arms into the Fortnite universe. Johnny Silverhand was quick on the draw to get his breathtaking image in the game, but Epic wants to bring the ever-terrifying Adam Smasher into Fortnite, so let’s find out where he is.

Adam Smasher and Fortnite are not a combination you’d automatically think of where crossovers are concerned. Yes, you do need to be of a certain persuasion where age is concerned to play the free-to-play shooter, but Adam Smasher’s look is unique…to say the least.

Let’s find out where the psychotic cyborg is roaming around if it isn’t in Fortnite.

Is Adam Smasher Skin in Fortnite?

Adam Smasher has not been added to Fortnite in the March 24 shop update as expected.

All the usual leakers in the Fortnite community outed the Adam Smasher skin well in advance. He was due to arrive toward the end of March, but due to unforeseen circumstances, the skin has been postponed.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite’s Huge Toy Story Collab Leaks Ahead of Release

There are some suggestions that the skin’s delay could be due to the Epic Games layoffs, but this remains speculation until we hear more.

When is Adam Smasher Coming To Fortnite? Release Period, Explained

Some leakers believe that Adam Smasher is due to arrive at some point in April 2026, but can’t pinpoint an exact date, whereas others believe it could be an indefinite delay.

You know something’s up when not even leakers know when a skin is dropping. The revised launch for the Adam Smasher content is up in the air as it stands, but it’s believed he will make his emphatic debut one way or another. With Crimson Desert & Fortnite collaborating, and other crossovers in the works, it’s got to be coming.

  • More Insider Gaming: Another Iconic Marvel Villain is Coming to Fortnite

As always, keep checking with Insider Gaming, as we will bring you confirmation of his arrival as soon as it’s announced by either Epic Games or if leakers get their hands on the elusive date.

Is Adam Smasher a day one buy for you? Does Epic need to bring any other Cyberpunk 2077 characters to the game, such as Solomon Reed from the Phantom Liberty DLC? Drop some suggestions via the Insider Gaming Discord.

If you missed it, check out all the fun Fortnite April Fools Update 2026 Content that dropped.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Gray Zone Warfare Console Release Planned for 1.0
item.content:encoded:
When Gray Zone Warfare came out in April 2024, MADFINGER Games made it clear that the eventual goal was to bring the open-world tactical shooter to console platforms. Yesterday, the game’s 0.4 update (named Spearhead) was released, bringing the most substantial evolution of the game yet, but there’s still no indication of when Gray Zone Warfare will come to consoles.

That is, until Rick Lagnese, PR Director at MADFINGER, took to social media to set out firmer expectations for when Gray Zone Warfare, which is now better than ever, is coming to console.

Gray Zone Warfare Will Be a Unique Addition to Consoles

Gray Zone Warfare is one of the best shooters out right now, and that’s represented by the growth it has seen in recent days. Since the 0.4 update was released, the game has skyrocketed up the charts on Steam, hitting a peak player count not seen since the game was launched in April 2024.

The review sentiments around the game are also improving greatly, with MADFINGER having made immense strides in the optimization and evolution of the title with the Spearhead update.

Following a few genre changes over the last two years, Gray Zone Warfare is now a PvE-first tactical sandbox shooter, which means it will be entering the console market with very little competition.

But when is Gray Zone Warfare coming to console?

When responding to a question on social media about that very topic, the PR Director at MADFINGER, Rick Lagnese, said:

Console released is planned for 1.0

Short, sharp, and simple. The roadmap for Gray Zone Warfare is long and stacked, and MADFINGER will want to pour further updates into the ecosystem before pushing it out to console platforms as a finished product.

It’s a similar path being followed by Escape from Tarkov, arguably the most prolific extraction shooter ever made. In November 2025, Escape from Tarkov hit 1.0, and the game’s console launch was addressed by the developer at Battlestate Games.

Gray Zone Warfare coming to consoles would mark the arrival of a massive, dense, and sumptuous open-world shooter with untold potential for expansion. That’s a huge win for console gamers.

Are you waiting for Gray Zone Warfare to arrive on console? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov has a wild April Fool’s Day task

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Nintendo’s Pokémon Summoning and Fighting Patent Rejected on a Non-Final Basis by USPTO
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo’s Pokémon patent for summoning characters and letting them fight for you has been rejected by the USPTO on a non-final basis.

The Nintendo and Palworld lawsuit has been ongoing for a long time, and the latest updates on the case indicate that Nintendo’s patents are under review. In October 2025, we saw an anti-Palworld patent rejected in Japan, and now the patent on summoning characters and letting them fight is out of reexamination and has been rejected.

Another Nintendo Patent Rejected in Palworld Lawsuit

A report by Gamesfray reveals that the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) has rejected Nintendo’s U.S. Patent No. 12,403,397 on a non-final basis. This comes after the reexamination process, following Nintendo’s missed deadline for the response. USPTO reached this conclusion based on a combination of several prior art references, one of which was Nintendo’s own 2019 patent.

The first prior art reference, Taura, was filed in 2019 and covers most of what the “summon-and-fight-in-1-of-2-modes” patent is about. This patent can be combined with Yabe and Motokura, thereby invalidating claims 1, 2, 5-7, 10-14, 17-19, and 22-26. The remaining 8 claims are covered by a 2020 patent by Bandai Namco and Shimomoto.

However, readers should note that the rejection is non-final, with Nintendo having two months to appeal and also an option to extend the period.

In other news, a new Nintendo patent may hint that DS titles are making their way to the Nintendo Switch. Additionally, the console maker accounts for over 50% of console sales in the UK. Here are more details. What are your thoughts on Nintendo’s Pokémon-summoning-and-fighting patent being rejected? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the latest addition to PlayStation Plus monthly games in April 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Escape from Tarkov’s April Fool’s Day Task is Pretty Nuts
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has updated Escape from Tarkov with a new quest made specially for April Fool’s Day, and it’s pretty insane. What’s hilarious is that, despite how unrealistically challenging it is, the Tarkov crowd is relishing the thought of completing it.

It’s a new task called Bucket of Nothing, and to complete it, you need to eliminate Reshala and his guards while using the ‘Blicky’, the toy gun that fires plastic discs. Battlestate has tried to help players by buffing the Blicky’s ammunition, but it’s still pretty damn tricky.

How to Complete Bucket of Nothing in Tarkov

If you’ve logged into Escape from Tarkov today, you’ll notice there’s a new task waiting for you from Prapor, called Bucket of Nothing. The task’s description has Prapor bagging on the player, suggesting they’re being lazy and not training properly, and for that, they need to venture onto Customs and eliminate Reshala and three of his guards.

But the catch is, they can’t just use any weapon.

They have to use ‘a Toy gun’, which in this case, is the Blicky, a meme gun added to Escape from Tarkov for April Fool’s Day in 2024.

It’s not impossible, though, thanks to Battlestate Games buffing the Blicky’s ammunition, boosting it from 1 damage point to 25 damage points. To put that into perspective, the weakest common round in Escape from Tarkov is probably the HK AP SX, which boasts just 35 damage per shot but has high penetration and armor damage.

To complete Bucket of Nothing, you’re best off running into Customs with a normal weapon and making Reshala and his guards as weak as possible without killing them. Then, aim for the head and let loose with the Blicky.

But what do you get for completing Bucket of Nothing? Here are the rewards:

  • 25,000 EXP
  • 236,000 Rubles
  • 1 x AK-308
  • 3 x AK-308 Mag
  • 7 x 20 pcs M61 Ammo

That’s not a bad reward. If you find yourself running low on Blicky supplies, you’ll be pleased to know that Battlestate Games has also tweaked airdrops, and when you open your next one, you might just be faced with dozens of toy guns.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve managed to complete Bucket of Nothing.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that only 600 players have hit max level in Escape from Tarkov

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: COD League ‘Most Financially Stable We’ve Ever Been’, Says Activision
item.content:encoded:
The Call of Duty League has, in some ways, had a rocky few years. It fell prey to the same issues that plagued almost every other facet of the esports industry for a while, becoming susceptible to the risky winter that threatened profitability, sustainability, interest, and investment. But, by all accounts, things are now firmly on the up.

In a recent interview with the General Manager of Call of Duty Esports, Daniel Tsay, Insider Gaming Esports learned just how bullish Activision is on the Call of Duty League, and for good reason. In Tsay’s words, things have never been better

Is the Call of Duty League Succeeding?

From an uninitiated perspective, it might not look as though the Call of Duty League is thriving. It’s not a top-tier esports title, but that’s not necessarily a problem. What is an issue is the ever-changing sentiment around each new annual release, some of the competitive elements of the game, and the stability of the wider Call of Duty ecosystem.

Despite these concerns, the Call of Duty League is, as Activision has directly suggested to Insider Gaming Esports, doing better than ever.

That’s from the horse’s mouth of Daniel Tsay, the General Manager of Call of Duty Esports. He has been in his role for several years, leading the competitive charge of Call of Duty across both Warzone and the traditional multiplayer titles, which at the time of writing was Black Ops 7.

Talking to Insider Gaming Esports, Tsay confirmed:

We’ve had to shift in how we execute, but we’ve really concentrated our bets into areas that I think have really propelled this forward.

We’re actually the most financially stable we’ve ever been. Viewership is also the highest it has ever been.

Tsay went on to talk about the prospect of taking the Call of Duty League Championship, the most valuable event in the calendar, out of North America and into Europe. If there’s one thing DreamHack Birmingham has proven, it’s that the Call of Duty esports scene is white-hot in the United Kingdom.

Do you believe that the Call of Duty League is doing better than ever? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that OpTic and FaZe have a killer match lined up at CDL Major 2

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: PlayStation Plus April 2026 Monthly Games Officially Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The PlayStation Plus monthly game additions for PS Plus Essential members have been officially confirmed.

Thanks to insider reports, players already had an idea about what to expect from PlayStation Plus Essential this month. First, it was revealed that Lords of the Fallen will be the headliner in April, and then the second game for the month was revealed soon after. Now, the official confirmation has arrived through Sony’s announcement.

PS Plus Essential Members Receive 3 Titles for April 2026 Monthly Games

The official blog post is out, confirming PlayStation Plus monthly games for April, which players can begin downloading from April 7, 2026, and add to their library until May 4.

First up is Lords of the Fallen, a soulslike action-RPG by CI Games, set in a medieval dark fantasy world. It’s the perfect time to try out the game, with the sequel, Lords of the Fallen 2, expected to arrive later this year for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC via Epic Games.

The second title is Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream, a perfect title for anime fans who are looking not only to experience a single-player story mode featuring an original story, but also to enjoy online modes with lobbies of over 20 players across five parties.

Last but not least, by any means, is Tomb Raider 1-3 Remastered, which brings the original three adventures together in one package, with all expansions and secret levels for modern platforms. It also features upgraded graphics, with the option to switch to original visuals and a new Challenge mode to “replay levels with customized modifiers and complete achievements to unlock 10 New Outfits that enhance Lara’s abilities.”

In other news, the prices of PlayStation 5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal devices are about to increase significantly worldwide this month. Additionally, a tech insider has claimed that the PS6 handheld will have a more powerful GPU than the Xbox Series. What are your thoughts on the PlayStation Plus monthly games for April? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Shift Up acquiring Resident Evil creator’s studio and publishing its upcoming games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Gears of War Voice Actor Says The Coalition Nearly Made Gears 6 Instead of E-Day
item.content:encoded:
Liam McIntyre brought JD Fenix to life in Gears of War 4 and 5. The voice actor has praised the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day showcase and the new direction for the franchise, but has revealed Gears 6 was happening, and he hopes to see the series continue.

Gears of War: E-Day is a 2026 prequel taking us all the way back to the events of Emergence Day and the fallout that ensued. It’s a bold move following recent sequels, and one of the series’ key voice actors has chimed in on Gears and where it was possibly headed.

Gears of War 6 Was Considered Before Gears of War: E-Day

JD Fenix voice actor Liam McIntyre recently responded to the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day presentation taking place after the Xbox Games Showcase.

He’s delighted about the announcement and revealed that he’s ‘pumped’ to see it happening.

When asked if he’s upset about the trilogy not being completed (Gears 6), here’s what he had to say on X:

“I hope they go back to it, but I love those guys and just want them to make a game they feel nails the gears experience everyone loves. That’d be awesome. And hey, if that leads back to the trilogy, Happy days.”

When quizzed about a ‘trilogy,’ McIntyre had an interesting response:

“We did Gears 4 and 5, and there was a suggestion that maybe there would be a Gears 6. But the team wanted to try something new as I understand it. And honestly, it looks great. Can’t wait to play.”

So, there was clearly a sense that The Coalition considered making Gears 6, but decided to go ahead with the Gears of War: E-Day prequel. Not many people are complaining, to be honest, and there’s a high likelihood that The Coalition goes back and develops Gears 6 anyway once E-Day is wrapped up!

Now it’s time for your say: Do you think The Coalition should’ve stayed the course and focused on Gears of War 6 first? Or was a fresh perspective and story with E-Day the right call? Let us know your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out rumors that PS6 handheld’s GPU is more powerful than the Xbox Series S, and the former Call of Duty director who wants to ‘Fix’ Fallout for more hot news.

MOBILE APP
